title: A Winter Without Snow: Why It Matters for the West
summary: In 2026, many parts of the Western United States had very little snow during winter. This is called a snow drought. Snow is important because it acts like a natural water storage, melting slowly in spring and summer to provide water for farms, cities, and nature. Without enough snow, there is less water available when it gets hot and dry. Ski resorts had to close early, and fire officials are worried about summer fires. Temperatures were warmer than usual, so rain fell instead of snow in some places. Water managers are telling people they might get less water than normal this year. This snow drought is a big problem for the West's water supply.

keywords.explanation: a time when there is much less snow than usual
the amount of water available for people and farms to use
how hot or cold it is outside
people who plan how to use and share water
places where people go to ski and play in the snow
snow that holds water until it melts

questions.question: What is a snow drought?
options: A time with too much snow
A time with much less snow than usual
questions.correct_answer: A time with much less snow than usual

questions.question: Why is snow important for the West?
options: It acts as natural water storage for summer
questions.correct_answer: It acts as natural water storage for summer

questions.question: What happened to ski resorts in 2026?
options: They had more snow than ever
They closed early due to lack of snow
They moved to a new location
questions.correct_answer: They closed early due to lack of snow

questions.question: Why are fire officials worried?
options: Because less snow means drier conditions in summer
questions.correct_answer: Because less snow means drier conditions in summer

questions.question: What did water managers say about water in 2026?
options: Some people might get less water than normal
Water will be free for all
There is no change in water supply
questions.correct_answer: Some people might get less water than normal

questions.question: What fell instead of snow in some places due to warm temperatures?

background_read: In the Western United States, snow that falls in mountains during winter is crucial because it melts slowly in spring and summer, providing water for drinking, farming, and nature. When there is less snow, it can lead to water shortages, making it hard for people and animals. This article talks about a year with very little snow, which is unusual and causes problems like early ski resort closures and concerns about fires.
Article_Structure: WHO: Water managers, fire officials, and people in the Western US
WHAT: A snow drought in 2026 with very little snow, affecting water supply
WHERE: The Western United States, including places like Idaho and Wyoming
WHY: Because warm temperatures led to rain instead of snow, reducing natural water storage for summer

perspectives.description: The snow drought shows how climate changes can harm water resources and ecosystems.
